explicit_text: I was born on the island of Dominica on February 6th. Growing up there was unique because the whole village helped raise children, and corporal punishment was common from anyone in the community. Life was simple and structured in its own way, with no street lights and a strict routine to be home before sunset. Both my parents were educators, which helped me excel in school, and my childhood was filled with playing games that often involved seasonal fruits. Although I spent a year living with my father to avoid being in my mother's class, that time was more about fun and life lessons than academics.
implicit_text: I was born on a Caribbean island on February 6th. Growing up there was unique because the whole village helped raise children, and corporal punishment was common from anyone in the community. Life was simple and structured in its own way, with no street lights and a strict routine to be home before sunset. Both my parents were educators, which helped me excel in school, and my childhood was filled with playing games that often involved seasonal fruits. Although I spent a year living with my father to avoid being in my mother's class, that time was more about fun and life lessons than academics.

explicit_text: After my time in Dominica, I moved to St. Croix, a smaller island north of Dominica that is part of the United States. The culture was slightly different, but the biggest change was how kids treated me once they found out where I was from. It was a tough period for me, being the new kid with many challenges. However, getting involved with the local church helped me find my place and enjoy life again. Eventually, I graduated from high school there before moving on to college.
implicit_text: After my time on that Caribbean island, I moved to a smaller island north of it that is part of the United States. The culture was slightly different, but the biggest change was how kids treated me once they found out where I was from. It was a tough period for me, being the new kid with many challenges. However, getting involved with the local church helped me find my place and enjoy life again. Eventually, I graduated from high school there before moving on to college.

explicit_text: Moving to the United States was a major turning point in my life. After graduating high school in St. Croix, which is part of the U.S., I decided to move to Georgia to pursue an engineering degree. The cultural and weather differences were striking, especially the hot winds in Atlanta compared to the cool island breeze I was used to. My plan was to get my degree and return home, but a hurricane severely damaged my island, and my mother suggested I stay in Atlanta. Over 30 years later, I am still here, married with children and a grandchild, working for a large company that lets me travel worldwide.
implicit_text: Moving to the mainland was a major turning point in my life. After graduating high school on that U.S. island, I decided to move to a southern state to pursue an engineering degree. The cultural and weather differences were striking, especially the hot winds in the city compared to the cool island breeze I was used to. My plan was to get my degree and return home, but a hurricane severely damaged my island, and my mother suggested I stay in the city. Over 30 years later, I am still here, married with children and a grandchild, working for a large company that lets me travel worldwide.

explicit_text: I chose to move to Georgia right after high school because I wanted to get away from home but not too far, and I aimed to become an engineer. Starting at DeVry University in Atlanta was a big adjustment, especially with the weather—the heat and hot winds were unlike anything I had experienced on the islands. I initially planned to finish my degree and return home, but after a hurricane damaged Dominica, I stayed in Georgia. Now, decades later, Georgia has become my home where I have built a family and a career.
implicit_text: I chose to move to a southern U.S. state right after high school because I wanted to get away from home but not too far, and I aimed to become an engineer. Starting at a technical university in the state's capital was a big adjustment, especially with the weather—the heat and hot winds were unlike anything I had experienced on the islands. I initially planned to finish my degree and return home, but after a hurricane damaged my Caribbean birthplace, I stayed in this state. Now, decades later, this southern state has become my home where I have built a family and a career.

explicit_text: I enrolled at DeVry University in Atlanta just two weeks before my high school graduation, eager to pursue engineering. The decision was rushed but necessary, as I wanted to move away from home and focus on my studies. DeVry became the place where I started my higher education journey, and although I had planned to return home after graduating, circumstances changed. The education I received there set the foundation for my career and life in the United States.
